channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Orlando and Chicago square off for conference showdown
item.description: Chicago Bulls (1-0, fourth in the Eastern Conference) vs. Orlando Magic (1-1, eighth in the Eastern Conference) Orlando, Florida; Saturday, 6 p.m. CDT BETMGM SPORTSBOOK LINE: Magic -5.5; over/under is 232.5 BOTTOM LINE: Chicago takes on Orlando for an Eastern Conference matchup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Barger, Varsho and Kirk lead homer barrage as Blue Jays rout Dodgers 11-4 in World Series opener
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Blue Jays fans waited 32 years to let loose cheering a World Series home run. They got to do it three times.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bichette returns from knee injury and sparks Blue Jays in World Series debut
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Favoring his injured knee and clearly not fully healed, Bo Bichette still managed to spark the Toronto Blue Jays in his World Series debut.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Blue Jays rookie Trey Yesavage holds down Dodgers for 4 innings in historic World Series start
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Standout rookie starter Trey Yesavage didn't have his best stuff but limited the Los Angeles Dodgers to two runs over four innings for the Toronto Blue Jays in Game 1 of the World Series on Friday night. Toronto rallied for an 11-4 win.
item.dc:creator: IAN HARRISON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jazz fall to Kings in final seconds, 105-104
item.description: SACRAMENTO (ABC4 Sports) - Coming off a surprising blowout victory in its season opener, the Utah Jazz looked poised to pull out an improbable road victory Friday night in Sacramento. But Domantis Sabonis scored off a rebound with 6.4 seconds left in a 105-104 victory over the Jazz.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Luka Doncic scores 49 to extend his sizzling season start in Lakers' 128-110 win over Timberwolves
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Luka Doncic scored 49 points and became the fourth player in NBA history to begin a season with back-to-back 40-point performances, leading the Los Angeles Lakers to a 128-110 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Blue Jays' Addison Barger hits first pinch-hit grand slam in World Series history
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Blue Jays outfielder Addison Barger launched the first pinch-hit grand slam in World Series history during Game 1 against the Los Angeles Dodgers on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: IAN HARRISON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trail Blazers take to the court for the first time after coach Chauncey Billups' arrest
item.description: Stunned by the arrest of head coach Chauncey Billups in a sprawling alleged poker scheme, the Portland Trail Blazers took to the court Friday night under an interim coach as they navigated an unexpectedly tumultuous start to the new season.
item.dc:creator: Anne M. Peterson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver 'deeply disturbed' by gambling arrests of Billups and Rozier
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver, in his first public comments since the arrests of Portland coach Chauncey Billups and Miami guard Terry Rozier on gambling-related charges, said Friday night that he was stunned by the indictments that have rocked the league.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ovechkin scores 899th goal as Capitals beat Blue Jackets 5-1 for 6th win in 7 games
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Alex Ovechkin scored his 899th career goal and added an assist, and the Washington Capitals beat the Columbus Blue Jackets 3-1 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Experts say criminal intent will be key in prosecuting NBA's Billups and Rozier in gambling cases
item.description: NBA coach and Hall of Fame member Chauncey Billups is charged with luring high-stakes poker players to games he knew were fixed, while veteran NBA forward Terry Rozier is accused of faking an injury and sitting on the bench to help bettors win thousands of dollars in 2023.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia responds to Olympic consequences for barring Israeli gymnasts from world championships
item.description: The IOC recommended no international sporting events be held in Indonesia after the country barred Israeli gymnasts from world championships in Jakarta.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bichette returns from knee injury and sparks Blue Jays in World Series debut
item.description: Bi Bichette singled early, walked to begin a nine-run sixth inning and made a nice defensive play in his first game at second base in six years, helping the Blue Jays beat the Los Angeles Dodgers 11-4 in the World Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Alex Vesia not with Dodgers in Toronto due to family matter
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Los Angeles Dodgers reliever Alex Vesia was not with the team in Toronto on Thursday, one day before Game 1 of the World Series, because of a “deeply personal family matter,” the Dodgers said in a statement posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chelsea's US private equity owners were once ridiculed. Now opinions are changing
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Against the geopolitical backdrop of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine in early 2022, a consortium led by American private equity titans took the opportunity to acquire one of soccer’s crown jewels. What followed at Chelsea was torrid and, at times, bewildering.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Blue Jays manager to Shohei Ohtani: We want our hat back
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — While most of baseball is saying hats off to Shohei Ohtani, Toronto manager John Schneider wants a cap back from the two-way star. Before signing a $700 million, 10-year contract with the Los Angeles Dodgers, the two-way star met with Blue Jays officials on Dec.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rookie Trey Yesavage to start World Series opener for Blue Jays, just his 7th big league game
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Five weeks after his major league debut, Trey Yesavage was summoned to Toronto manager John Schneider's office, where the 22-year-old found Blue Jays pitching coach Pete Walker also waiting for him.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA betting scandal includes wagers made on Chicago Bulls game from 2023
item.description: The recent NBA betting scandal includes wagers made on a Chicago Bulls game from 2023, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: NBA All-Star Jimmy Butler joins San Diego sports world as minority investor
item.description: Jimmy Butler, six-time NBA All-Star, has joined San Diego Wave FC as a Minority Investor and aims to set a new standard for women's sports in San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Sharisse Cohee, Fox 5

item.title: A look at notable sports betting scandals
item.description: Dozens of players, coaches and officials have been accused or charged in gambling-related cases across major leagues.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Former Alabama QB AJ McCarron announces run for Lieutenant Governor
item.description: McCarron was with the Tide for five years, winning back-to-back National Championships under coach Nick Saban.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Matteson, NewsNation

item.title: Bear it all?: Wieners Circle to give away free hot dogs if Ben Johnson goes shirtless after a win
item.description: CHICAGO — Last month, an iconic Chicago hot dog stand on the North Side dished out dozens of free hot dogs after Bears quarterback Caleb Williams threw four touchdown passes, and the chance could soon come again for those who missed out on the hot dog extravaganza.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cuypers scores 2 goals and the Fire advance out of the wild-card round with 3-1 victory over Orlando City
item.description: Hugo Cuypers scored two second-half goals, Brian Gutiérrez added another and the Chicago Fire advanced out of the wild-card round of the MLS Cup playoffs with a 3-1 victory over Orlando City on Wednesday night at SeatGeek Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz crush Clippers in season opener, 129-108
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - This was not how the Utah Jazz season was supposed to start. Expected to be one of the worst teams in the NBA, the Jazz came out and crushed the veteran-laden L.A. Clippers in the season opener Wednesday night at the Delta Center, 129-108.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Big 12 drops BYU fine, Crumbl CEO puts money toward players, paying off school lunch debt
item.description: Crumbl's CEO, Jason McGowan, shared that the Big 12 conference will not be fining BYU for fans rushing the field after the team's victory over the Utah Utes last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Ava Hart, ABC 4

item.title: Golf legend Jack Nicklaus wins $50M verdict in defamation lawsuit against former business partners
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Professional golf legend Jack Nicklaus has won a $50 million verdict in a defamation lawsuit filed in Florida against his former business partners.
item.dc:creator: DAVID FISCHER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michael Jordan is going to be part of NBC's NBA coverage. His reason: to give back to the game
item.description: Once upon a time, Michael Jordan was the unquestioned No. 1 star player in the "NBA on NBC" universe. A generation of stars later, Jordan plans to contribute his considerable insights into NBC's return to broadcasting the league.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NBA rosters contain a record-setting 135 international players
item.description: Basketball has exploded in global popularity over the past decades, and now every team has at least one international player on its roster.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: NASCAR reveals course layout for San Diego race in 2026
item.description: NASCAR unveiled the course layout and racetrack map for next year's highly anticipated race weekend in America's Finest City.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: New Utah Jazz practice facility coming to the Shops at South Town
item.description: Smith Entertainment Group (SEG) announced that a new Utah Jazz practice facility is coming to the Shops at South Town in Sandy.
item.dc:creator: Ava Hart, ABC 4
